name: First State Bank
description_short: Kentucky-based community bank offering personal and business banking services, including mortgages, construction loans, and certificates of deposit through the First Breckinridge Bancshares network.
description_long:
First State Bank is a community-focused financial institution operating as part of the First Breckinridge Bancshares family, which collectively serves communities across Kentucky and Southern Indiana through a portfolio of seven banks. The bank emphasizes local banking relationships and personal service, positioning itself as a regional alternative to larger national institutions. The website tagline "We listen, then we lend" reflects their stated approach to customer relationships.

First State Bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal and business banking products. On the personal side, they provide checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (currently advertising 12-month CDs at 3.85% APY and 7-month CDs at 3.42% APY), and a financial management tool called Insights. Their lending products include mortgages for home purchases and refinancing, bridge loans to facilitate home transitions, and construction financing. For businesses of all sizes, they offer cash management services and business banking solutions.

The bank distinguishes itself through its local community presence and emphasis on relationship banking. They maintain multiple physical branch locations across Kentucky and provide phone support (1.800.290.7893). The company also publishes financial education content through their blog, featuring articles on topics like debt paydown, retirement planning, and emergency savings. Their integration into the First Breckinridge Bancshares network provides additional institutional resources while maintaining local decision-making.

As a traditional community bank, First State Bank is best suited for customers seeking personalized service and local branch access rather than digital-first banking. The website content does not provide information about fees, minimum account balances, credit card offerings, or detailed loan terms, which limits transparency for prospective customers. While they advertise competitive CD rates, their full range of products, pricing, and approval criteria remain unclear from the available information.
pros: Part of established First Breckinridge Bancshares network with seven banks across Kentucky and Southern Indiana
Competitive CD rates advertised (3.85% APY for 12-month, 3.42% APY for 7-month CDs)
Multiple physical branch locations for in-person banking and relationship management
Diverse lending products including mortgages, bridge loans, and construction financing
Free financial management tool (Insights) to help customers organize finances
Cash management services specifically designed for businesses of all sizes
Educational content and financial planning articles available to customers
cons: Limited geographic footprint (Kentucky and Southern Indiana only) compared to national banks
Website lacks specific information about account fees, minimum balances, or interest rates on checking/savings accounts
No mention of credit card products, overdraft protection details, or mobile banking capabilities
Loan approval criteria, terms, and rates not transparently published on website
Limited details about online banking features beyond login access
best_for: Kentucky and Southern Indiana residents seeking local banking relationships and personal service
Small to medium-sized businesses needing cash management and construction financing
Customers prioritizing branch access and phone support over digital-only banking
Homebuyers and refinancers in their service area looking for portfolio lenders
diagnosis: First State Bank is best for Kentucky and Southern Indiana residents who value local community banking relationships, personalized service, and branch access over digital convenience. The primary caveat is the lack of transparent pricing and fee information on their website—prospective customers must contact the bank directly to understand account costs, minimum balances, and full loan terms.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
